title: Tropical Islands from Berlin: Quick Summary
items: **Tropical Islands is a 26°C indoor rainforest resort inside one of the world's largest free-standing halls**, about 60 km south of Berlin near Krausnick. The Aerium hangar is 360 m long, 210 m wide and 107 m high, built in 2000 for CargoLifter airships and reopened as a tropical resort on 19 December 2004.

**Getting there is a normal regional train ride**: RE2 or RB24 to Brand Tropical Islands, about 45 minutes from Berlin Ostkreuz, then a free shuttle bus timed to the trains. The €63/month Deutschlandticket covers the whole ride; groups can use the €36.50 Brandenburg-Berlin-Ticket for up to 5 people.

**In 2026, board at Ostkreuz.** The Stadtbahn is closed to regional trains from 14 June to 12 December 2026, so the RE2 runs via Gesundbrunnen, Ostkreuz and Schöneweide instead of Hauptbahnhof's upper platforms.

**Book the ticket online before you go.** Day tickets start around €34.90 online for adults but cost €55.90 or more at the door. Children up to three are free, the day runs 08:00 to 23:30, and the textile-free Sauna & Spa area costs extra.

**It is the strongest rainy-day rescue in the Berlin area** and a favourite with families: a 200 m sandy beach, a 27 m slide tower, a real indoor rainforest with around 50,000 plants, and the AMAZONIA outdoor pools in summer.
